questions.question: In a certain code CHARM is coded as SNDIB and BLISS is coded as TTCMJ. Then what will be the code for CHALK?
questions.explanation: The word is divided in two groups, group of 3 and group of 2, and both groups interchange their position. Then alphabets replaced with the next alphabets in the alphabetical series. CHA LK = LK CHA LK CHA= ML DIB

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The square of the 1st and 2nd digits are added to get the sum 1+9=10 1+25= 26 and not 28 1+16=17 4+9= 13

questions.question: Which answer figure will replace the question mark in question figure? Question figure: - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6565/image006.png) Answer figure: - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6565/image007.png)
questions.explanation: The triangle is replaced by square and square is replaced by pentagon, in the first analogy. Applying the same logic: Option a is the correct answer.

questions.question: Arrange the words given below in a meaningful sequence. 1. Order 2. Eat 3. Menu 4. Bill 5. Restaurant
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: 53124. Restaurant, menu, order, eat, bill.

questions.question: In a certain coded language MAN is coded as 82 and JUG= 83 then which of the following will be the code for HEN?
questions.explanation: If we assign position values to alphabets such that A= 1, B=2, C= 3 and so on, then to get the code, the sum of the position values is reversed to get the code M=13, A=1, N=14; 13+1+14= 28, reversed= 82 J=10, U=21, G=7; 10+21+7=38, reversed= 83 H=8, E=5, N=14; 8+5+14= 27, reversed= 72

questions.question: Select the set of numbers that is not similar to the following set of numbers. (32, 48, 64)
questions.explanation: The logic is, all the numbers in any set, except in option (c), are multiples of 8, such that, from left to right they are alternate multiples of 8. In give set → 4*8 = 32; (4+2)*8= 6*8= 48 and 8*8= 64 Similarly, 3*8 = 24; 5*8 = 40; 7*8= 56 7*8= 56; 9*8= 72; 11*8= 88 11*8= 88; 13*8= 104; 15*8= 120

questions.explanation: All grandfathers are fathers and, some grandfather as well as some father are brothers. Hence, IV. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p7432/image031.jpg)

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. BROT : UNSA ∷ DKLF : ?
questions.explanation: BROT → TORB T+1=U O-1=N R+1=S B-1=A Similarly, DKLF → FLKD F+1=G L-1=K K+1=L D-1=C DKLF : GKLC

questions.question: How many pentagons are there in the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p7482/image001.jpg)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p7482/image002.jpg) Pentagons in the given figure are - ABCFD, ABECD, AJECD, BCFGE, CDHIE, CDFGE, CFGIE, DFGIH, DHLJA, EJLHI Hence, 10 pentagons.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: D + 10 = N + 10 = X B + 10 = L + 10 = V F + 10 = P + 10 = Z But, J + 7 = Q + 8 = Y, which makes this option odd.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except bear, all the other are herbivores. Bear is a carnivore.

questions.question: In the following question, from the given alternative words, select the word which can be formed using the letters of the given word. JOURNALISM
questions.explanation: Only MAJOR can be formed.

questions.question: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two numbers on same side. 7 + 16 ÷ 8 X 2 + 1 = 3 X 42 ÷ 6 - 9 + 5
questions.explanation: On interchanging 2 and 7 on LHS, we get, = 2 + 16 ÷ 8 X 7 + 1 = 2 + 14 + 1 = 17 RHS = 3 X 7 - 9 + 5 = 21 - 4 = 17

questions.question: Select the related word from the qiven alternatives BATON: MUSIC CONDUCTOR :: ? : JUDGE
questions.explanation: Baton is a type of stick used by a music conductor. Similarly a court judge uses a short hammer which is also called a Gavel.

questions.question: A is mother of B and B is mother of C. C is daughter of D and D is brother of E. F is son of E and G is sister of F and daughter of H. How is D related to F?
questions.explanation: Let us draw the family tree according to the above given conditions, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/prelimsimg/7233/image080.jpg) D is uncle of F.

questions.question: Each of the questions below consists of a question followed by five options. You have to decide which option is correct: Nine persons M, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T and U are sitting in a row facing north. Q sits third to the left of U, who sits second to right of M. T sits third to right of S, who sits second to right of N. R sits third to right of M and N sits third to left of Q. P sits at one end immediate right of R. Who are sitting between O and T?
questions.explanation: We have: Q sits third to left of U and U sits second to right of M. R sits third to right of M. P must sit at right most as P sits immediate right of R. T sits third to right of S and N sits third to left of Q. Based on above data we get the following result: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1683/image017.jpg) Thus, S, Q and M are sitting between O and T.

questions.question: Which of the following options will complete the given series: C _ A _ _ R _ C _ R _ B _ _ A C
questions.explanation: The series is: CRAB BRAC CRAB BRAC

questions.question: The total ages of A and B is 30 Years more than the present age of C. If the present age of B is 10 years more than the present age of C, what is the present age of A?
questions.explanation: Let the present ages of A, B and C be A, B and C respectively. As per the given conditions => Present ages of A+B = C + 30→① => Present age of B = C+10→② => Using ① and ② => A + C + 10 = C+30 => A = 20 years

questions.question: In a certain code if 'M' denotes'÷', 'N' denotes 'X', 'O' denotes '+' and 'P' denotes '-', then what will be the value of 15M3N6O5P21O5?

In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some locks are hacks. All hacks are charts. Conclusions: I. Some hacks are locks. II. Some charts are hacks.
options: If either Conclusion I or II follows.
If both Conclusions I and II follow.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/syllogism/image0031068_locks_hacks.jpg) Both conclusions follow

questions.question: Find the next term in the series. 189, 220, 263, 320, 393, ?
In a certain code 'CLEAR' is coded as '39261' and 'TURBINE' is coded as '7514802'. In the same code how will 'CELLULAR' be coded as?
questions.explanation: The code is as follows:CLEARTUBIN 3926175480 Thus, the code for CELLULAR will be 32995961
